instruction: Enable a strict quiet mode: disable notification banners and disable lock-screen notifications. Then create ~/Desktop/dnd_status.txt with two lines exactly: show-banners=false and show-in-lock-screen=false.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Turn off or adjust notifications (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/shell-notifications.html.en)

parameters.command: gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.notifications show-banners true; gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.notifications show-in-lock-screen true; rm -f ~/Desktop/dnd_status.txt

result.command: b=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.notifications show-banners 2>/dev/null); l=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.notifications show-in-lock-screen 2>/dev/null); expected=$(printf 'show-banners=false\nshow-in-lock-screen=false\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/dnd_status.txt 2>/dev/null); file_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && file_ok='true'; echo "show_banners=$b"; echo "show_in_lock_screen=$l"; echo "file_ok=$file_ok"
